title: Creamy Herbal Irish Kielbasa Hash
description: A lazy‑Sunday Irish hash that marries smoky kielbasa with creamy herbs, perfect for a low‑carb weekend meal. The dish is quick, comforting, and packs a punch of flavor with a gentle citrusy finish.
instructions: Slice the kielbasa into ¼‑inch thick rounds.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat; add olive oil and butter, letting butter melt.
Add the sliced kielbasa and brown on both sides, about 4–5 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, pour in the diced onion and bell pepper; sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the kielbasa to the skillet, reduce heat to low, and pour in heavy cream.
Stir gently, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly, about 3–4 minutes.
Sprinkle crumbled feta, chopped parsley, and thyme, then season with salt and pepper; cook for another 2 minutes until everything is heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with extra parsley if desired.
